app_title: SIUT Pediatric Cardiothoracic Division Hospital Survey
thank_you: Thank you for completing the survey.
offline_notice: You are offline. Your response will be saved and submitted when connection is restored.
section_admission: When Your Child Was Admitted to This Hospital
section_care_after_admission: Your Child's Care After Admission to This Hospital
section_care_note: Do not include care received in the Emergency Room for the rest of the survey.
section_child_nurses_note: The next questions ask about your child's experience with nurses and doctors. Your own experience will be asked later.
section_providers_note: A provider means a doctor, nurse, nurse practitioner, or physician assistant.
section_child_care: Your Child's Care in This Hospital
section_discharge: When Your Child Left This Hospital
section_teen: Your Teen's Care in This Hospital
q1: For this hospital stay, was your child admitted through this hospital's Emergency Room?
q2: Were you in this hospital's Emergency Room with your child?
q3: While your child was in this hospital's Emergency Room, were you kept informed about what was being done for your child?
q4: During the first day of this hospital stay, were you asked to list or review all of the prescription medicines your child was taking?
q5: During the first day of this hospital stay, were you asked to list or review all of the vitamins, herbal medicines, and over-the-counter medicines your child was taking at home?
q6: Is your child able to talk with nurses and doctors about his or her health care?
q7: How often did your child's nurses listen carefully to your child?
q8: How often did your child's nurses explain things in a way that was easy for your child to understand?
q9: How often did your child's nurses encourage your child to ask questions?
q10: How often did your child's doctors listen carefully to your child?
q11: How often did your child's doctors explain things in a way that was easy for your child to understand?
q12: How often did your child's doctors encourage your child to ask questions?
q13: How often did your child's nurses listen carefully to you?
q14: How often did your child's nurses explain things to you in a way that was easy to understand?
q15: How often did your child's nurses treat you with courtesy and respect?
q16: How often did your child's doctors listen carefully to you?
q17: How often did your child's doctors explain things to you in a way that was easy to understand?
q18: How often did your child's doctors treat you with courtesy and respect?
q19: How often were you given as much privacy as you wanted when discussing your child's care with providers?
q20: Did providers ask you about things the family knows best about the child (how child usually acts, what makes child comfortable, how to calm fears)?
q21: How often did providers talk with and act toward your child in a way that was right for your child's age?
q22: How often did providers keep you informed about what was being done for your child?
q23: Did your child have any tests (blood tests, x-rays, etc.) during this hospital stay?
q24: How often did providers give you as much information as you wanted about the results of these tests?
q25: How often did the staff come to help when your child called out for assistance?
q26: Was your child given any medicine during this hospital stay?
q27: Did providers/staff tell you how to report concerns about mistakes in your child's health care (e.g. wrong medicine, wrong surgery)?
q28: Did your child have pain that needed medicine or other treatment during this hospital stay?
q29: How often did providers/staff ask about your child's pain as often as your child needed?
q30: How often were your child's room and bathroom kept clean?
q31: How often was the area around your child's room quiet at night?
q32: Did the hospital have age-appropriate things available for your child (toys, books, mobiles, games)?
q33: Before your child left, did a provider ask you if you had concerns about whether your child was ready to leave?
q34: Before your child left, did a provider talk with you as much as you wanted about how to care for your child's health after leaving?
q35: Before your child left, did a provider tell you that your child should take any new medicine not taken before this hospital stay?
q36: Before your child left, did a provider/pharmacist explain clearly how your child should take these new medicines after leaving?
q37: Before your child left, did a provider/pharmacist explain clearly possible side effects of these new medicines?
q38: Did a provider explain clearly when your child could return to regular activities (eating, bathing, school, sports)?
q39: Did a provider explain clearly what symptoms/health problems to look out for after leaving?
q40: Did you get information in writing about what symptoms/health problems to look out for after leaving?
q41: During this hospital stay, was your child 13 years old or older?
q42: During this hospital stay, how often did providers involve your child in discussions about his or her health care?
q43: Before your child left the hospital, did a provider ask your child if he or she had any concerns about whether he or she was ready to leave?
q44: Before your child left the hospital, did a provider talk with your child about how to take care of his or her health after leaving the hospital?
q45: Using any number from 0 to 10, where 0 is the worst hospital possible and 10 is the best hospital possible, what number would you use to rate this hospital during your child's stay?
q46: Would you recommend this hospital to your friends and family?
q47: In general, how would you rate your child's overall health?
q49: Is your child male or female?
q50: How are you related to the child?
q52: What is the highest grade or level of school you have completed?
q54: During your child's hospital stay, how much of the time were you at the hospital?
q55: Is there anything else you would like to say about the care your child received during this hospital stay?
q56: Did someone help you complete this survey?
q57: How did that person help you? (Mark one or more)
edu_some_hs: Some high school, but did not graduate
all_of_the_time: All or nearly all of the time
help_wrote: Wrote down the answers I gave
help_translated: Translated the questions into my language
